headline: Sabah and Sarawak receive 15% of federal development funds despite 60% of landmass
context: Sabah and Sarawak collectively received only 15% of federal development allocation in 2025 despite comprising 60% of Malaysia's total land area and 20% of population.

cards.big: Sabah and Sarawak: 60% of Malaysia's land, 20% of people, but only 15% of development funds
cards.sub: Borneo states systematically underfunded relative to size, need, and contribution

cards.big: MA63 promised equitable development — 63 years later, allocation remains disproportionate
cards.sub: Constitutional commitment to Borneo development has not translated into fiscal reality

cards.big: Sabah's poverty rate of 19.5% is triple the national average of 6.2%
cards.sub: Chronic underfunding correlates with Malaysia's worst development indicators

cards.big: Federal development formula weighs population over area, disadvantaging large rural states
cards.sub: Allocation methodology structurally discriminates against states with dispersed populations

cards.big: MA63 was meant to be a partnership — 60 years of underfunding looks more like extraction
Should the development allocation formula be revised to account for area and infrastructure deficit?
